entities.name: SEED DETERIORATION
entities.description: A continuous process of quality loss in seeds

entities.name: LONGEVITY
entities.description: The duration for which seeds remain viable

entities.name: SEED MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The percentage of water in seeds, specifically 3-7% for preservation

entities.name: STORAGE TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature at which seeds are stored, specifically below 0°C

entities.name: LONG-TERM PRESERVATION
entities.description: The process of maintaining germplasm samples over a long period

entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: Genetic resources stored as seed samples

entities.name: SEED COAT CRACKS
entities.description: Physical fissures in the seed coat caused by rapid drying

entities.name: IMBIBITION INJURY
entities.description: Damage occurring when dried seeds absorb water too quickly

relationships.description: Combined with storage temperature, it is suitable for preservation

Undergo a continuous process of deterioration
Can develop cracks if dried too rapidly
Dried seeds are susceptible to these injuries
Increases susceptibility to injury during rapid water absorption
Techniques are needed to prolong longevity against deterioration
